MYERS NURSING & CONVALESCENT CENTER

CCN: 26E084 · KANSAS CITY, MO 64127 · Jackson County

Overview

Address
2315 WALROND AVENUE, KANSAS CITY, MO 64127

8162313180
Certified beds

Avg daily residents
73 (87% of beds filled)

For-profit LLC
Provider type

Medicare/Medicaid since
1974-10-01
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Footnote 24.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 1.57 2.67 2.27 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.06 0.47 0.08 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.46 0.59 0.67 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.05 1.61 1.52 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 0.52 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
1.31
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.04
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
1.89
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
61.5%
annual
RN turnover
Staffing penalized (missing PBJ submission).
Administrators departed
3
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
0.94
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.69
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-10-10 12 11 1 80 0 80
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-12-08 30 27 4 232 1 232
Total weighted health score
118.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (66)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
65 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2025-10-10 2025-11-21
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2025-10-10 2025-11-21
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-10-10 2025-11-21
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-10-10 2025-11-21
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-10-10 2025-11-21
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2025-10-10 2025-11-25
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-10-10 2025-11-21
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-10-10 2025-11-21
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. E 2025-10-10 2025-11-25
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2025-10-10 2025-11-21
0923 Have enough outside ventilation via a window or mechanical ventilation, or both. E 2025-10-10 2025-11-21
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-06-04 2025-07-14
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2024-05-31 2024-07-02
0626 Permit a resident to return to the nursing home after hospitalization or therapeutic leave that exceeds bed-hold policy. D 2024-05-31 2024-07-02
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0661 Ensure necessary information is communicated to the resident, and receiving health care provider at the time of a planned discharge. D 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0678 Provide basic life support, including CPR, prior to the arrival of emergency medical personnel , subject to physician orders and the resident’s advance directives. E 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. E 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. F 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. F 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. E 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. F 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. D 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0851 Electronically submit to CMS complete and accurate direct care staffing information, based on payroll and other verifiable and auditable data. F 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. E 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. E 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. E 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2023-12-08 2024-01-22
0564 Inform each resident of his or her visitation rights and ensure that all visitors enjoy equal visitation privileges. D 2023-08-14 2023-09-22
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2022-06-08 2022-07-22
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2022-06-08 2022-07-22
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2022-06-08 2022-07-22
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2022-06-08 2022-07-22
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. E 2022-06-08 2022-07-22
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2022-06-08 2022-07-22
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. D 2022-06-08 2022-07-22
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2022-06-08 2022-07-22

Showing 50 most recent of 66. See the All Data CSV for the full list.

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
